does: This paper proposes MISP-DPO, a framework that extends Direct Preference Optimization to vision-language models by incorporating multiple semantically diverse negative images rather than single negatives. The method embeds prompts and candidate images in CLIP space and uses sparse autoencoders to identify interpretable semantic deviations. Negative samples are selected based on reconstruction difficulty, semantic deviation from the positive image, and mutual diversity among negatives. The framework adopts a Plackett-Luce model to handle multi-negative comparisons and introduces an importance sampling strategy to improve training efficiency, aiming to reduce optimization bias and hallucinations in multimodal preference learning.
connects: This work builds on Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), which itself extended preference learning from reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) into a simpler supervised setting. The paper responds to recent extensions of DPO to vision-language models that relied on oversimplified pairwise comparisons. It connects to broader research on learning from human preferences in multimodal settings and leverages CLIP embeddings, a foundational technique in contrastive vision-language learning. The use of sparse autoencoders for semantic interpretation builds on interpretability research in representation learning, while the Plackett-Luce model extends ranking-based preference models to handle richer comparative structures than standard pairwise methods.
enables: This framework opens pathways for more sophisticated preference learning in multimodal models by demonstrating that richer negative sampling strategies reduce hallucinations and optimization bias. The importance sampling approach could be adapted to other preference optimization methods and multimodal architectures beyond vision-language models. The interpretable semantic factors discovered via sparse autoencoders may enable better understanding and control of vision-language model behavior. Future work could explore how multi-negative DPO generalizes to longer preference chains, combines with other alignment techniques like RLHF, or scales to larger collections of candidate images, potentially improving the robustness and reliability of multimodal AI systems in practical applications.
